roads.summary: Multiple mobility disruptions are active, driven by severe weather damage and corridor closures rather than a single national flashpoint.

evidence.title: South Africa declares natural disaster as flooding kills at least 10
evidence.note: Flooding affected six provinces and temporary closures were ordered in the Western Cape.

evidence.title: Sanral announces rolling N3 closures for blasting near Market Road
evidence.note: Scheduled N3 closures affect a major freight corridor.

evidence.title: Road closures be implemented for Freedom Day celebrations
evidence.note: Official event-driven closures are still relevant because they constrain alternate routes.

fuel.summary: Price stress is high, but current official statements are reassuring on domestic supply continuity.

evidence.title: Mineral and Petroleum Resources reassures South Africans of stable fuel supply amid global energy uncertainty
evidence.matchedQuery: South Africa fuel supply prices government
evidence.note: Government said consignments were secured and domestic supply remains the priority.

evidence.title: National Treasury on extension of short-term relief measures to address fuel price increases
evidence.note: Relief measures confirm price pressure, but not widespread retail unavailability.

political-triggers.summary: Some parliamentary and public-order pressure signals exist, but there is no single nationally dominant trigger loaded in this snapshot.

evidence.title: Media Statement: Committee Resolves To Request an Extension for Gang-Related Violence Inquiry
evidence.matchedQuery: South Africa court ruling protest parliament
evidence.note: This is not a mass-mobilisation trigger by itself, but it is a live institutional stress signal.

mobilization.summary: Organised mobilisation is present through taxi-strike language and anti-immigration protests across multiple locations.

evidence.title: Long-distance taxi strike put on hold after permit talks progress
evidence.note: The strike was paused, which helps, but the mobilisation machinery clearly exists.

evidence.title: Taxi bosses plan mass strike over Durban ban
evidence.note: Taxi-sector escalation matters because it can flip commuter mobility fast.

evidence.title: 130 Nigerians seek repatriation after latest anti-immigration protests in South Africa
evidence.note: Confirms multi-city protest energy with international spillover.

police-capacity.summary: There is no clean nationwide evidence of systemic failure, but live inquiry and operation reporting suggest continued pressure on policing bandwidth.

evidence.title: Media Statement: Committee Resolves To Request an Extension for Gang-Related Violence Inquiry
evidence.note: Parliament extending a gang-violence inquiry is a meaningful pressure signal.

evidence.title: South African Police Service in Limpopo continues to tighten the grip on crime through Operation Shanela
evidence.note: Official operations continue, which cuts against a pure collapse narrative.

local-protests.summary: Several localised protest and road-block stories are current enough to matter operationally, even if they do not add up to a national shutdown.

evidence.title: Taxi strike brings major Klerksdorp roads to a standstill
evidence.matchedQuery: South Africa local protest road blocked
evidence.note: A good example of how local disruptions become immediate household movement problems.

evidence.title: Media Statement: COGTA Committee Condemns Violent Kugompo Protests and Reaffirms Rule of Law
evidence.matchedQuery: South Africa service delivery protest road closure
evidence.note: Violent local protest action still matters even when it is geographically narrow.

food-pharmacy.summary: This snapshot does not show strong evidence of broad retail or pharmacy availability failure.

firearms.summary: Firearm-related policing signals are active, with recent seizure reporting and continued official attention to removing weapons from circulation.

evidence.title: Operation Lockdown III members seize three firearms in Nyanga
evidence.note: The seizure included an R5 rifle and ammunition.

evidence.title: SAPS destroys 13 859 firearms during its second firearm destruction process
evidence.note: This is a control measure, not a threat spike, but it keeps weapons circulation in view.

misinfo.summary: Misinformation is currently a live operational issue, especially around fake xenophobia and panic-content circulation.

evidence.title: Cabinet sounds alarm on fake images and videos about attacks on foreign nationals
evidence.matchedQuery: South Africa fake videos misinformation protests
evidence.note: Cabinet directly addressed manipulated content linked to current protest narratives.

evidence.title: WhatsApp crime groups spreading misinformation and fuelling fear in KZN
evidence.matchedQuery: South Africa WhatsApp panic fake news
evidence.note: A reminder that local panic channels create real behavioural effects.
